flying_page.title: Flying Bird
flying_page.subtitle: A cross-platform mobile clone of the classic Flappy Bird, optimized for Android devices.

about.title: Mobile Development Journey
desc: Flying Bird is a personal project developed to explore the mobile pipeline within Unreal Engine.
As a solo developer, I handled the entire process: from C++ gameplay logic and procedural spawning to Android-specific configurations like touch inputs and icon management.
The goal was to create a polished, lightweight experience that maintains consistent performance across both mobile and desktop platforms.

gameplay.title: Hard-to-Master Gameplay
items: • Physics-Based Movement: Precise impulse-based logic for the bird's flight, tuned for responsive feedback on touchscreens.
• Infinite Spawning: A procedural system that generates and recycles obstacles (pipes) to create a seamless infinite loop.
• Adaptive Difficulty: Obstacle placement and gaps designed to provide a challenging but fair progression.
• Audio Feedback: Integrated sound system with real-time mute functionality and saved preferences.

tech.title: The Mobile Pipeline
items: • Android Integration: Managed the full deployment pipeline, including SDK/NDK setup, Android manifests, and custom icon sets.
• C++ Spawner Logic: Developed a dedicated PipesSpawner class to manage the lifecycle of obstacles efficiently.
• SaveGame System: Implementation of a local persistence layer for high scores and audio settings using C++.
• Input Scalability: Unified input mapping that handles both screen taps (Android) and mouse clicks/keyboard (Windows).

systems.title: UX & UI Polish
items: • Mobile HUD: A simplified UI featuring a pre-game countdown and a dynamic scoring system tailored for small screens.
• State Management: Clean transition logic between the main menu, gameplay loop, and the game-over screen.
• Performance Tuning: Optimized textures and materials to ensure high frame rates on a wide range of Android devices.

game_state.title: Event-Driven Game State
game_state.desc: The Bird Game State (C++) acts as the central authority for game rules. It utilizes Dynamic Multicast Delegates to decouple gameplay events (Score, Dead, Pause) from the UI and other actors.
game_state.img1: Broadcast Logic: Breakdown of the delegate system. By broadcasting events like OnStartGame, the system ensures a clean communication flow between the countdown timer and the physics activation.

bird.title: Physics & Velocity Control
bird.desc: Custom flight logic implemented in the Bird class. To ensure consistent jump height, the system resets vertical velocity before applying each new impulse force.
bird.img1: Impulse Handling: Analysis of the ApplyImpulse function, showing the velocity normalization technique and air resistance damping (Linear Damping) setup.

pipes.title: Collision & Score Detection
pipes.desc: The Pipes actor manages complex collision filtering. It distinguishes between lethal obstacles (the pipe meshes) and the scoring trigger zone through specialized Trace Channels.
pipes.img1: Collision Profiles: Configuration of custom object types (BIRD, PIPE, SCORE) to handle overlapping events and death triggers accurately.

spawner.title: Procedural Offsetting
spawner.desc: The Pipes Spawner manages the infinite loop using a tick-based delay system and FFloatRange for vertical randomization.
spawner.img1: Spawner Configuration: Real-time generation of obstacles with randomized Z-axis positioning to maintain an unpredictable challenge.

input.title: Enhanced Input System
input.desc: Implementation of UE5 Enhanced Input for cross-platform support. It maps actions to both spacebar/mouse-click for Windows and screen-taps for Android.
input.img1: Mapping Context: The IMC_Input setup showing unified key bindings for a consistent feel across devices.

ui.title: Logic-Driven & Cross-Platform UI
ui.desc: A sophisticated UI system where every Widget Blueprint is parented to a C++ class. It features platform-specific logic and programmatic styling to ensure performance and visual consistency.
ui.img1: Cross-Platform Optimization: Implementation of conditional UI elements using #if PLATFORM_ANDROID. The pause button is dynamically injected only on mobile builds, maintaining a clean HUD on desktop.
ui.img2: Programmatic Slate Styling: Analysis of the AudioMute logic. Button states (Normal, Hover, Pressed) and textures are managed directly via C++ using FSlateBrush for optimized state switching.
ui.img3: Architecture & Binding: Usage of meta = (BindWidget) to safely link C++ logic to UI elements, coupled with NativeConstruct for efficient event delegation.

cta.title: Ready to fly?
cta.subtitle: Download the APK for Android or the Windows version on itch.io.
